description:
The .envrc (added in while-youre-out-lk6 fix) adds ~/.pub-cache/bin to PATH so melos works in local shells. This should be documented in the README or a CONTRIBUTING.md.

## What to add
- README section: 'Local Development Setup' explaining:
  1. Run 'dart pub global activate melos' to install melos
  2. Either: add ~/.pub-cache/bin to your shell PATH, OR use direnv with the .envrc provided
  3. When running melos run test locally without a TTY, use '--no-select' flag: 'melos run test --no-select'
- Note that CI works fine because GitHub Actions runner adds pub-cache/bin to PATH automatically after 'dart pub global activate melos'

## Why
Without this, developers get 'melos: command not found' when running melos scripts, which is confusing.
title: Document melos local dev setup: PATH and --no-select flag

description:
## Meta
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Platform | Flutter (iOS + Android) |
| Privacy model | On-device only — no network, no cloud |

---

## Problem statement
Users need to remember context-specific tasks when they arrive at a physical location (e.g. grocery items at a store, errands at a hardware store). Existing todo apps are not location-aware. Existing location-aware apps require cloud accounts and sacrifice privacy.

## Goal
A native-feeling, fully private mobile app that lets users attach todo lists to geographic locations and receive a local notification when they enter that location's boundary.

---

## Architecture

### Layer stack (top → bottom, strict dependency direction)
```
UI (Flutter widgets)
  └── ViewModel (screen state, no business logic)
        └── Domain (use cases, pure Dart, zero dependencies)
              └── Repository (abstracts all data sources)
                    ├── SQLite via drift (todos, lists, geofences)
                    ├── OS Geofence API (Core Location / GeofencingClient)
                    └── OS Notification API (UNUserNotification / NotificationManagerCompat)
```

### Package structure (`packages/`)
| Package | Depends on | Key libs |
|---|---|---|
| `domain` | nothing | pure Dart |
| `data` | `domain` | drift, sqflite |
| `geofencing` | `domain` | flutter_background_geolocation OR platform channels |
| `notifications` | `domain` | flutter_local_notifications |
| `ui_kit` | nothing | Flutter widgets, theme |
| `apps/mobile` | all packages | Flutter app shell |

### Data model (SQLite, on-device)
```
todo_list        { id, title, color, notify_on_enter, notify_on_exit, geofence_id FK, sort_order, created_at }
todo_item        { id, list_id FK, title, notes, is_done, due_date, priority }
geofence_region  { id, latitude, longitude, radius_m, label, is_active, trigger, created_at }
```
Index: `todo_item(list_id, is_done)` for fast incomplete-item counts.

### Geofence event flow
```
User sets location on list
  → RegisterGeofenceUseCase → write to SQLite + register with OS API
  → [user moves] → OS fires region entry event → wakes app in background
  → GeofenceEventHandler → query SQLite for matched list → post local notification
  → Tap notification → deep-link to list by listId (passed in notification payload)
```

### iOS geofence limits + mitigation
- Hard OS limit: 20 simultaneous monitored regions
- Strategy: priority queue sorted by distance from current location; swap nearest 20 active regions as user moves using `CLLocationManager.distanceFilter`
- Background mode: `location` with `allowsBackgroundLocationUpdates = true`, `pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically = true`
- Minimum radius: 100m

### Android geofence
- Limit: 100 simultaneous regions via `GeofencingClient`
- Events delivered via `PendingIntent` → `BroadcastReceiver` → `WorkManager`

---

## Use cases (domain layer)
| Use case | Description |
|---|---|
| `CreateListUseCase` | Create a named todo list with optional color |
| `DeleteListUseCase` | Delete list, items, and associated geofence |
| `AssignLocationUseCase` | Attach a lat/lng + radius to a list |
| `RegisterGeofenceUseCase` | Write geofence to DB + register with OS |
| `UnregisterGeofenceUseCase` | Remove from DB + deregister from OS |
| `ToggleItemUseCase` | Mark todo item done/undone |
| `ReorderListsUseCase` | Update sort_order for drag-and-drop |
| `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase` | Query matched list, build + post notification |
| `RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase` | Re-evaluate priority queue on location update |

---

## Screens
| Screen | Key behaviours |
|---|---|
| List dashboard | Shows all lists, incomplete item count badge, tap → detail |
| List detail | CRUD todo items, shows attached location chip |
| Map picker | Full-screen map, tap to place pin, drag slider for radius |
| Location detail | View/edit attached geofence, toggle notify on enter/exit |
| Notification settings | Per-list notification prefs, quiet hours |
| Permissions onboarding | Explains always-on location need before system prompt |

---

## CI / release pipeline

### Repo
- Single monorepo, tool: `melos`
- `melos.yaml` at root; workflows at `.github/workflows/`

### CI (`ci.yml`) — triggers: push + PR to `main`/`develop`
```
analyze (ubuntu)
  ├── test:domain (ubuntu, parallel)
  ├── test:data (ubuntu, parallel)
  └── test:geofencing (ubuntu, parallel)
        └── coverage (merge lcov → Codecov)
              ├── build:ios (macos-14, push only)
              └── build:android (ubuntu, push only)
```

### Release (`release.yml`) — trigger: `v*.*.*` tag push
```
release-ios   → sign IPA → TestFlight
release-android → sign AAB → Play Store internal track
github-release  → auto-generate release notes from tag
```

### Required GitHub secrets
`IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_P12`, `IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_PASSWORD`, `APPSTORE_ISSUER_ID`, `APPSTORE_API_KEY_ID`, `APPSTORE_API_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD`, `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS`, `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD`, `PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`

---

## Build phases

### Phase 1 — Core CRUD
- Scaffold monorepo, melos, CI skeleton
- `domain` package: entities + use cases (no location yet)
- `data` package: drift schema, migrations, repositories
- `ui_kit`: design system, theme, base widgets
- List dashboard + list detail screens (no map)
- Full unit test coverage on `domain` and `data`

### Phase 2 — Location assignment
- Map picker screen (flutter_map or google_maps_flutter)
- `AssignLocationUseCase` + `RegisterGeofenceUseCase`
- Geofence written to DB, registered with OS
- Location permissions onboarding flow
- No background events yet — validate registration only

### Phase 3 — Background events + notifications
- `geofencing` package: platform bridge or flutter_background_geolocation
- `GeofenceEventHandler` wired to `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase`
- `notifications` package: local notification posting + deep-link routing
- iOS priority queue + region swapping (`RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase`)
- End-to-end test: enter region → notification fires → tap → correct list opens

### Phase 4 — Polish + release
- Notification grouping, snooze, quiet hours
- List sort/reorder drag-and-drop
- Empty states, permission denial handling, edge case UX
- Release workflow: TestFlight + Play Store internal
- Performance audit: geofence handler must complete within 10s iOS background budget

---

## Key constraints
- Zero network calls at runtime — all data on-device
- SQLite file in app private documents directory, excluded from iCloud backup (`isExcludedFromBackup = true`)
- Always-on location permission required; must be justified in `Info.plist` usage string and in onboarding UI
- Background handler budget: ≤10s on iOS
- Bundle ID: `com.yourcompany.localist` (update before Phase 4)
title: While You're Out  — Location-Aware Todo App

description: DashboardViewModel already exposes reorderLists(List<String> orderedIds) which calls ReorderListsUseCase. The DashboardScreen currently uses a plain ListView with no drag handles. In Phase 4 (polish), replace it with ReorderableListView so users can reorder their lists by dragging. The ViewModel method is already wired up and ready. Part of epic: while-youre-out-1pq. See apps/mobile/lib/screens/dashboard/dashboard_screen.dart
title: Add drag-to-reorder support on DashboardScreen (Phase 4)

description:
The onboarding flow (apps/mobile/lib/screens/onboarding/permissions_onboarding_screen.dart) only requests location permissions (when-in-use and always-on). It does NOT request notification permission, which is required for local notifications to display.

On iOS, FlutterNotificationService.requestPermission() handles this via flutter_local_notifications (IOSFlutterLocalNotificationsPlugin.requestPermissions). On Android 13+, POST_NOTIFICATIONS must be explicitly requested via permission_handler (Permission.notifications.request()).

A third onboarding page should be added after the 'always-on location' step that:
1. Explains why notifications are needed ('We'll notify you when you arrive at a saved location')
2. Calls notificationServiceProvider.notificationService.requestPermission() (or Permission.notification.request() for Android 13+)
3. Handles the denied case gracefully with an informational message

Part of epic: while-youre-out-1pq. Discovered while implementing while-youre-out-v3r.
title: Add notification permission step to PermissionsOnboardingScreen

description:
In apps/mobile/lib/main.dart, notificationTapCallback is registered before notificationService.initialize() so that cold-start payloads (app launched by tapping a notification) are forwarded immediately.

However, the callback calls router.go('/list/$listId') where router is obtained via ref.read(appRouterProvider). The appRouterProvider depends on sharedPreferencesProvider (a FutureProvider). If SharedPreferences hasn't resolved yet at the point the cold-start callback fires inside initialize(), the router's redirect logic may not have the 'onboarding_complete' flag and could redirect to /onboarding instead of the intended list.

Verify the timing on a real device:
1. Post a notification via debug trigger
2. Kill the app
3. Tap the notification — confirm the correct list screen opens, not onboarding

If the race condition is confirmed, the fix is to await the sharedPreferencesProvider future before calling router.go, or guard the navigation with a post-frame callback.

Part of epic: while-youre-out-1pq. Discovered while implementing while-youre-out-v3r.
title: Cold-start deep-link: verify router is ready before notificationTapCallback fires

close_reason: Implemented full data package: drift schema (3 tables), AppDatabase with FK pragma + composite index, data mappers, 3 repository implementations (DriftTodoListRepository, DriftTodoItemRepository, DriftGeofenceRepository), 27 passing tests, dart analyze: 0 issues, generated .g.dart committed
title: Phase 1: data package — drift schema, migrations, and repository implementations

close_reason: Implemented full domain package: 4 entities, 3 repo interfaces, 2 service interfaces, 11 use cases, 34 passing tests, dart analyze: 0 issues
title: Phase 1: domain package — entities, repository interfaces, and use cases

description:
The current GeofenceEventHandler (apps/mobile/lib/geofence_event_handler.dart) subscribes to GeofenceService.events at the Flutter/Dart layer. This works when the app is in the foreground or background (active process), but Android kills the app process when it is in the background for too long.

For reliable Android background execution, the native GeofencingClient BroadcastReceiver (added in while-youre-out-7mq) should dispatch a WorkManager OneTimeWorkRequest when a geofence event is received. The WorkRequest's doWork() implementation should call back into Flutter via a MethodChannel to invoke HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase, or alternatively call the SQLite DB and post the notification entirely from native code.

This is a Phase 4 hardening task. The current implementation is acceptable for development and simulator testing but may miss events on production Android devices where the OS aggressively manages background processes.

Part of epic: while-youre-out-1pq. Discovered while implementing while-youre-out-v3r.
title: Android: wire GeofenceEventHandler background dispatch via WorkManager

description:
There is a TODO comment in packages/data/lib/src/database/app_database.dart inside _openConnection() noting that the SQLite file should be excluded from iCloud backup by setting NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey on the file URL.

This is deferred to Phase 2 iOS native work as mentioned in the TODO comment. The implementation requires a platform channel call from apps/mobile after the DB file is created.

See: packages/data/lib/src/database/app_database.dart — TODO(phase-2-ios) comment

Part of the epic: while-youre-out-1pq
title: iOS: exclude DB file from iCloud backup (NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey)

close_reason: Duplicate of while-youre-out-u42 which covers the same fix.
description:
The melos.yaml test:domain script uses 'flutter test --coverage' but the domain package is a pure Dart package with no flutter SDK dep. Running 'flutter test' on it will either fail or not generate useful coverage data. Fix: change the test:domain script (and test:data if data is also pure Dart) to use 'dart test --coverage' instead.

Also: 'dart pub global run coverage:format_coverage' is not activated globally, so lcov.info never gets populated. Either activate the coverage package globally in CI or use a different coverage aggregation approach.

Related: the melos coverage:combine script may also need updating.

Part of the Phase 1 quality-gates work.
title: Fix melos test:domain script — use dart test instead of flutter test for pure-Dart package

close_reason: Implemented: RealGeofenceService via platform channels, iOS CLLocationManager handler, Android GeofencingClient handler, GeofenceManager iOS priority-queue swap, all wired in DI. 3 tests pass, analyze clean.
title: Phase 3: geofencing package — OS platform bridge (iOS + Android)

description:
The while-youre-out-2tm acceptance criteria stated melos test:data should pass with >=90% coverage. Actual coverage (excluding .g.dart) is ~75% because:

1. tables.dart — Drift column-definition lambdas (e.g. text()(), integer()()) are metadata-only and not exercised at runtime; they appear as uncovered lines.
2. app_database.dart — The production _openConnection() path uses path_provider (not available in unit tests), leaving those lines uncovered.

Options to close the gap:
- Use a coverage filter (e.g. lcov --remove) to exclude generated/platform-only files from the coverage report
- Add an integration test that uses the real file-backed DB (requires a device/emulator)
- Accept the current coverage as sufficient for a data-layer package where generated and platform-specific paths are inherently untestable in pure unit tests

This is a documentation/tooling follow-up, not a functional bug. The business logic (repositories, mappers, database schema) is fully tested.
title: data package: coverage below 90% acceptance criterion due to generated/platform code

description:
## Overview
Polish pass for Phase 4: drag-and-drop list reordering, notification quiet hours, notification grouping on Android, Location Detail screen, permission-denial recovery, empty states audit, list title/color editing, and a performance audit of the geofence background handler.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-v3r (end-to-end geofence → notification pipeline must be working)

## Feature 1: Drag-and-drop list reordering (apps/mobile/lib/screens/dashboard/)

Replace `ListView.builder` in `DashboardScreen` with `ReorderableListView.builder`:
```dart
ReorderableListView.builder(
  onReorder: (int oldIndex, int newIndex) {
    if (newIndex > oldIndex) newIndex--;  // adjust for removal
    viewModel.reorderLists(oldIndex, newIndex);
  },
  itemCount: lists.length,
  itemBuilder: (context, index) => AppListTile(
    key: ValueKey(lists[index].id),
    ...
    trailing: ReorderableDragStartListener(
      index: index,
      child: const Icon(Icons.drag_handle),
    ),
  ),
)
```

`DashboardViewModel.reorderLists(int oldIndex, int newIndex)`:
1. Reorder the local list copy
2. Extract ordered IDs
3. Call `ReorderListsUseCase(orderedIds)`
4. `notifyListeners()`

## Feature 2: Location Detail screen (apps/mobile/lib/screens/location_detail/)

This screen was specified in the epic but not yet implemented. Add it as a route reachable from `ListDetailScreen` → location chip tap (instead of going directly to the map picker).

Route: `/list/:listId/location`

`LocationDetailScreen` shows:
- Map thumbnail (non-interactive mini FlutterMap, centered on the geofence, with the radius circle overlay)
- Geofence label (editable inline text field)
- Radius value (read-only summary: "200 m")
- Two toggles: "Notify on arrival" (`notifyOnEnter`) and "Notify on departure" (`notifyOnExit`) — these update the `TodoList` record via `saveList`
- "Edit Location" button → navigates to `/list/:listId/map` (MapPickerScreen)
- "Remove Location" button → calls `UnregisterGeofenceUseCase` + clears `list.geofenceId`, pops back

Update `ListDetailScreen` location chip tap to navigate to `/list/:listId/location` instead of directly to the map picker.

## Feature 3: Notification grouping (Android — packages/notifications/)

In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`:
```dart
const androidDetails = AndroidNotificationDetails(
  'geofence_alerts',
  'Location Reminders',
  channelDescription: 'Notifications when you arrive at a saved location',
  importance: Importance.high,
  priority: Priority.high,
  groupKey: 'com.yourcompany.whileyoureout.geofence_group',
);
```

After posting an individual notification, count active geofence notifications and post a summary if >1:
```dart
// Summary notification (Android only)
if (Platform.isAndroid) {
  await _plugin.show(
    0,  // fixed ID for the summary
    'While You\'re Out',
    'Multiple location reminders',
    const NotificationDetails(
      android: AndroidNotificationDetails(
        'geofence_alerts',
        'Location Reminders',
        groupKey: 'com.yourcompany.whileyoureout.geofence_group',
        setAsGroupSummary: true,
      ),
    ),
  );
}
```

## Feature 4: Quiet hours (apps/mobile/lib/screens/notification_settings/)

`NotificationSettingsScreen` at route `/settings/notifications`:
- `SwitchListTile` for "Enable quiet hours" — persisted via `shared_preferences` key `'quiet_hours_enabled'`
- Start time picker (default 22:00) — persisted as `'quiet_hours_start'` (int minutes since midnight, default 1320)
- End time picker (default 07:00) — persisted as `'quiet_hours_end'` (int minutes since midnight, default 420)
- Use Flutter's `showTimePicker` dialog

Tap pickers: show `showTimePicker()`, save selected time.

Access from Dashboard AppBar overflow menu (⋮):
```dart
PopupMenuButton(
  itemBuilder: (_) => [
    const PopupMenuItem(value: 'settings', child: Text('Notification settings')),
  ],
  onSelected: (value) {
    if (value == 'settings') context.push('/settings/notifications');
  },
)
```

In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`:
```dart
final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
final quietEnabled = prefs.getBool('quiet_hours_enabled') ?? false;
if (quietEnabled) {
  final now = TimeOfDay.now();
  final nowMinutes = now.hour * 60 + now.minute;
  final start = prefs.getInt('quiet_hours_start') ?? 1320;
  final end = prefs.getInt('quiet_hours_end') ?? 420;
  final inQuietHours = start > end
      ? (nowMinutes >= start || nowMinutes < end)  // overnight range
      : (nowMinutes >= start && nowMinutes < end);
  if (inQuietHours) return;  // suppress silently
}
```

## Feature 5: Permission denial handling (apps/mobile/lib/screens/)

In both `MapPickerScreen` and `PermissionsOnboardingScreen`:

**Permanently denied (locationWhenInUse):**
```dart
final status = await Permission.locationWhenInUse.status;
if (status.isPermanentlyDenied) {
  showDialog(context: context, builder: (_) => AlertDialog(
    title: const Text('Location permission required'),
    content: const Text('Please enable location access in your device Settings.'),
    actions: [
      TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context), child: const Text('Cancel')),
      TextButton(onPressed: () { openAppSettings(); Navigator.pop(context); },
                 child: const Text('Open Settings')),
    ],
  ));
  return;
}
```

**Always denied but When-In-Use granted:**
In `ListDetailScreen`, add a `MaterialBanner` (persistent, closeable with "Fix" action) when:
- `list.geofenceId != null`
- `Permission.locationAlways.status == PermissionStatus.denied || .limited`
```dart
MaterialBanner(
  content: const Text('Background location denied — arrival reminders won\'t work.'),
  actions: [
    TextButton(onPressed: openAppSettings, child: const Text('Fix')),
    TextButton(onPressed: () => ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).hideCurrentMaterialBanner(),
               child: const Text('Dismiss')),
  ],
)
```

## Feature 6: Empty states audit

Verify every screen has its `AppEmptyState` implemented:
| Screen | Icon | Headline | Body |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dashboard | `Icons.checklist` | "No lists yet" | "Tap + to create your first list" |
| List detail | `Icons.edit_note` | "Nothing here yet" | "Add your first item below" |
| Map picker (no pin placed) | `Icons.location_on_outlined` | "Tap the map" | "Drop a pin to set your reminder location" |

## Feature 7: List title and color editing

In `ListDetailScreen` AppBar, add an edit icon (`Icons.edit_outlined`):
- Opens `EditListBottomSheet` (modal bottom sheet):
  - `TextFormField` pre-filled with current title, max 50 chars, auto-focus
  - `ColorPickerRow` pre-selected to current color
  - "Save" button → `listRepo.saveList(list.copyWith(title: newTitle, color: newColor))`
  - "Cancel" button

## Feature 8: Performance audit

Wrap `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase` call in `GeofenceEventHandler` with a stopwatch in debug mode:
```dart
assert(() {
  final sw = Stopwatch()..start();
  handleEntry(event.regionId).then((_) {
    debugPrint('[Geofence] HandleEntry completed in ${sw.elapsedMilliseconds}ms');
    assert(sw.elapsedMilliseconds < 8000,
      'HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase exceeded 8s iOS background budget!');
  });
  return true;
}());
```

## Tests
- `DashboardViewModel.reorderLists`: verify `ReorderListsUseCase` called with correct ordered ID list after a reorder
- `LocationDetailScreen`: verify toggles update `notifyOnEnter`/`notifyOnExit`; "Edit Location" navigates to map picker
- `FlutterNotificationService`: quiet hours in range → `show` NOT called; quiet hours overnight range; outside range → `show` IS called
- `PermissionDenial`: permanently denied → AlertDialog shown; always denied + geofence attached → MaterialBanner shown in ListDetail

## Acceptance criteria
- Drag-and-drop reorder persists across app restarts
- Location Detail screen shows geofence info, toggles work
- Quiet hours suppress notifications during the configured window (including overnight wraparound)
- Permission denial flows do not crash the app and provide a path to fix
- All empty states render correctly on fresh install
- List title + color editing works end-to-end
- `melos analyze` passes with zero warnings
title: Phase 4: Polish — drag-and-drop reorder, quiet hours, notification grouping, empty states, and edge case UX

close_reason: Implemented full ui_kit package: AppColors, AppTextStyles, AppTheme (light+dark, Material 3), AppScaffold, AppListTile, AppCheckbox (animated strikethrough), AppEmptyState, AppBottomSheet, ColorPickerRow, RadiusSlider. 20 widget tests passing, dart analyze: 0 issues.
description:
## Overview
Implement the `packages/ui_kit` package: the app's design system, theme, reusable base widgets, and color constants.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-qys (monorepo scaffold)

## Package location
`packages/ui_kit/`

## Key dependencies (pubspec.yaml)
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

## Theme (lib/src/theme/)

### `AppTheme`
- Provide static getters: `AppTheme.light` → `ThemeData`, `AppTheme.dark` → `ThemeData`
- Use Material 3 (`useMaterial3: true`)
- Color seed: the same blue used as default list color (`0xFF2196F3`)
- Define `AppColors` constants class with the palette used across the app:
  - `AppColors.listColors` — list of 8 ARGB int values users can pick for list color
  - `AppColors.danger` — red for delete actions

## Typography (lib/src/theme/text_styles.dart)
Define `AppTextStyles` with named text style constants:
- `AppTextStyles.listTitle` — 18sp, medium weight
- `AppTextStyles.itemTitle` — 16sp, regular
- `AppTextStyles.badge` — 12sp, bold, for incomplete count badge
- `AppTextStyles.caption` — 12sp, muted, for dates/secondary info

## Base widgets (lib/src/widgets/)

### `AppScaffold`
Wraps `Scaffold` with consistent AppBar styling. Props: title, body, actions, floatingActionButton.

### `AppListTile`
Styled list tile with:
- Leading color dot (circle, 12px diameter, filled with list color)
- Title text
- Trailing badge showing incomplete item count (hidden if 0)
- Location pin icon if list has geofence attached

### `AppCheckbox`
Custom checkbox widget with strikethrough animation on todo items.

### `AppEmptyState`
Centered illustration + headline + body copy. Props: icon (IconData), headline (String), body (String), action (optional Widget).

### `AppBottomSheet`
Convenience wrapper for `showModalBottomSheet` with consistent padding and drag handle.

### `ColorPickerRow`
Horizontal scrollable row of 8 color circles. Tapping one selects it (checkmark overlay). Emits `onColorChanged(int color)`.

### `RadiusSlider`
A labeled slider for geofence radius. Range: 100m – 5000m. Shows formatted label: '250 m', '1.2 km'. Emits `onRadiusChanged(double meters)`.

## Barrel export
`lib/ui_kit.dart` — export AppTheme, AppColors, AppTextStyles, and all widgets.

## Tests
Widget tests for `AppCheckbox` (tap toggles), `ColorPickerRow` (tap fires callback), `RadiusSlider` (emits value in range), `AppListTile` (badge hidden when count = 0, shown when > 0).

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos analyze` passes
- All widgets have dartdoc comments
- Widget tests pass
title: Phase 1: ui_kit package — design system, theme, and base widgets

close_reason: Duplicate of while-youre-out-05d — same title and description. Closing the less-detailed copy.
description: The .envrc (added in while-youre-out-lk6 fix) adds ~/.pub-cache/bin to PATH so melos works in local shells. This should be documented in the README or a CONTRIBUTING.md. Add a Local Development Setup section explaining: (1) run dart pub global activate melos, (2) add ~/.pub-cache/bin to PATH or use direnv with the .envrc, (3) use melos run test --no-select when running without a TTY.
title: Document melos local dev setup: PATH and --no-select flag

close_reason: Fixed: added .idea/, *.iml, melos_*.iml, and pubspec_overrides.yaml to .gitignore. Removed all previously-tracked IDE and melos-generated files from git index with git rm --cached.
description:
During the monorepo scaffold (while-youre-out-qys), .idea/ run configurations and *.iml files were committed because they are not excluded in .gitignore.

## What to do
Add the following to the root .gitignore:
- .idea/
- *.iml
- melos_*.iml

Also consider adding pubspec_overrides.yaml to .gitignore since those are melos-generated local overrides and should not be committed.
title: Add .idea/ and *.iml to .gitignore to avoid committing IDE files

description:
## Overview
Finalize the app for release: update the bundle ID, configure Android keystore signing, add the iOS ExportOptions.plist, verify the release workflows, and document all required GitHub Secrets.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-946 (all polish and UX work must be complete)

## Part A — Bundle ID finalization

The epic uses `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout` in the CI workflows but the epic description notes bundle ID is `com.yourcompany.localist` — **use `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout` everywhere** (the CI/release workflows already use this). Update if any file still references `localist`.

**iOS:**
- `apps/mobile/ios/Runner.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`: set `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER = com.yourcompany.whileyoureout;` for all targets/configurations
- `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/Info.plist`: verify `CFBundleIdentifier = $(PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER)`

**Android:**
- `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle`: `applicationId "com.yourcompany.whileyoureout"`

## Part B — iOS ExportOptions.plist

Create `apps/mobile/ios/ExportOptions.plist` (referenced in `release.yml`):
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>method</key>
  <string>app-store</string>
  <key>teamID</key>
  <string>REPLACE_WITH_TEAM_ID</string>
  <key>uploadBitcode</key>
  <false/>
  <key>compileBitcode</key>
  <false/>
  <key>signingStyle</key>
  <string>automatic</string>
  <key>destination</key>
  <string>upload</string>
</dict>
</plist>
```
Note: `teamID` must be replaced with the real Apple Developer Team ID before the first TestFlight release.

## Part C — Android keystore signing config

In `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle`, add signing config that reads from environment variables (injected by the CI workflow):
```groovy
android {
  signingConfigs {
    release {
      storeFile file('../app/whileyoureout.jks')   // decoded from secret in CI
      storePassword System.getenv('KEYSTORE_PASSWORD') ?: ''
      keyAlias System.getenv('KEY_ALIAS') ?: ''
      keyPassword System.getenv('KEY_PASSWORD') ?: ''
    }
  }
  buildTypes {
    release {
      signingConfig signingConfigs.release
      minifyEnabled true
      proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile('proguard-android-optimize.txt'), 'proguard-rules.pro'
    }
  }
}
```

Add `apps/mobile/android/app/whileyoureout.jks` to `.gitignore` (the file is decoded from the CI secret at build time and must never be committed).

## Part D — verify release workflows

The `.github/workflows/release.yml` already exists and is fully configured. Verify these details match the actual project:
- IPA path: `apps/mobile/build/ios/ipa/whileyoureout.ipa` ← confirm this matches what `flutter build ipa` produces (it may be `Runner.ipa` depending on the scheme name; adjust if needed)
- AAB path: `apps/mobile/build/app/outputs/bundle/release/app-release.aab` ← standard Flutter path, should be correct
- Package name in Play Store upload step: `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`

## Part E — required GitHub Secrets documentation

Add a `docs/RELEASE_SETUP.md` file to the repo explaining how to configure each required secret:

| Secret | How to obtain |
|---|---|
| `IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_P12` | Export from Keychain Access as .p12, base64-encode: `base64 -i cert.p12` |
| `IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_PASSWORD` | Password used when exporting the .p12 |
| `APPSTORE_ISSUER_ID` | App Store Connect → Users and Access → Keys → Issuer ID |
| `APPSTORE_API_KEY_ID` | App Store Connect → Users and Access → Keys → Key ID |
| `APPSTORE_API_PRIVATE_KEY` | Content of the .p8 file downloaded from App Store Connect |
| `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64` | `base64 -i whileyoureout.jks` |
| `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD` | Password used when creating the keystore |
| `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS` | Alias used when creating the keystore |
| `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD` | Key password (often same as keystore password) |
| `PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` | Google Cloud service account JSON with Play Developer API access |

Also document: minimum App Store Connect and Google Play Console setup steps (app registration, privacy policy URL, age rating) needed before first upload.

## Acceptance criteria
- `flutter build ipa --no-codesign` succeeds locally from `apps/mobile/`
- `flutter build appbundle --release` succeeds locally (using test keystore)
- `release.yml` trigger on a `v0.1.0` tag runs without workflow errors (can be tested with a dry-run tag on a branch)
- `docs/RELEASE_SETUP.md` exists and is complete
- Bundle ID is consistent across all platform files
title: Phase 4: Release pipeline — TestFlight, Play Store, GitHub Secrets, and bundle ID finalization

close_reason: Implemented and committed in b16d2ab. Remaining repo-wide melos analyze blockers tracked in while-youre-out-63l.
title: Phase 2: Map picker screen and location assignment

description: The general 'melos run test' script now uses packageFilters (dirExists: test) and failFast, but requires --no-select to run non-interactively. The current CI workflows correctly avoid this by calling individual scoped scripts (melos test:domain, melos test:data, etc.) rather than melos run test. Consider adding a single 'melos run test --no-select' step to CI or keeping the per-package approach. Either is fine, but should be a deliberate decision. If melos run test --no-select is used, it must come after melos bootstrap.
title: Update CI workflows to use 'melos run test --no-select' for the general test script

description: melos analyze exits with code 1 due to info-level lint violations in packages/data/test/data_test.dart and packages/ui_kit. Issues are pre-existing (not introduced by Phase 1 work) and include: avoid_redundant_argument_values, require_trailing_commas. These are not errors but melos analyze treats any issue as a failure. Fix: resolve all info violations in both packages so 'melos analyze' exits 0 across the whole monorepo. Part of epic: while-youre-out-1pq
title: Fix pre-existing melos analyze info violations in data and ui_kit packages

close_reason: Fixed: rewrote all melos scripts to use exec: key (melos v6 idiomatic) instead of 'run: melos exec ...' to avoid dependency on melos being in PATH. Added failFast: true and packageFilters: dirExists: test for the test script. Added .envrc to prepend ~/.pub-cache/bin to PATH. Verified melos run test --no-select works non-interactively.
description:
The melos.yaml 'test' script (melos run test) triggers an interactive package-selection prompt in non-TTY environments (CI, agent shells), causing it to crash with:

  StdinException: Error getting terminal echo mode

## Root cause
The melos exec --fail-fast in the test script is valid, but melos run test itself prompts the user to select a package when run without a filter.

## Fix
Update the 'test' script in melos.yaml to add packageFilters so it runs across all packages non-interactively, similar to how the analyze/format scripts work. For example:

  test:
    run: melos exec --fail-fast -- flutter test --coverage
    description: Run all unit and widget tests across all packages.
    packageFilters:
      dirExists: test

Verify by running: melos run test (should execute without prompting).

Note: domain uses 'dart test' not 'flutter test' — verify the flutter test command works for pure-Dart packages too (it does via the Flutter SDK's test runner).
title: Fix melos 'test' script — non-interactive execution fails with interactive prompt

description:
melos.yaml specifies melos: ^6.0.0 in bootstrap.dev_dependencies, but the installed version is 7.5.0, which required a root pubspec.yaml to recognize the workspace.

## What to do
Update melos.yaml:
  command:
    bootstrap:
      dev_dependencies:
        melos: ^7.0.0

Also document in README that a root pubspec.yaml is required for melos 7.x workspace discovery.
title: Update melos version constraint in melos.yaml bootstrap.dev_dependencies

close_reason: Implemented Phase 1 list dashboard and list detail screens. DashboardScreen + DashboardViewModel, ListDetailScreen + ListDetailViewModel, CreateListBottomSheet, go_router config, Riverpod providers. 20 widget tests passing, flutter analyze clean.
description:
## Overview
Implement the two main screens for Phase 1: the List Dashboard (home screen) and the List Detail screen (CRUD todo items). No map or geofencing in this phase.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-3r5 (domain package)
- while-youre-out-2tm (data package with repositories)
- while-youre-out-aci (ui_kit package)

## Package location
`apps/mobile/lib/`

## Architecture pattern
Use ViewModel per screen. ViewModels use `ChangeNotifier` or `Riverpod` (choose one — Riverpod recommended, using `riverpod: ^2.5.0` + `flutter_riverpod: ^2.5.0`). Use cases are injected via Provider/Riverpod provider.

## Dependency injection
In `apps/mobile/lib/main.dart`:
- Instantiate `AppDatabase`
- Instantiate concrete repository implementations (`DriftTodoListRepository`, etc.)
- Instantiate use cases with injected repositories
- Expose use cases via Riverpod providers (or `InheritedWidget` if not using Riverpod)

## Screen 1: List Dashboard (`lib/screens/dashboard/`)

### `DashboardScreen`
- Route: `/` (initial route)
- Watches `watchAllLists()` from domain (reactive stream)
- Displays a ListView of `AppListTile` widgets, one per TodoList
- Each tile shows: color dot, title, incomplete item count badge, location pin icon if geofenceId != null
- FAB opens `CreateListBottomSheet`
- Long-press on tile shows delete confirmation dialog (calls `DeleteListUseCase`)
- Tap on tile navigates to List Detail screen
- Empty state: use `AppEmptyState` with a checklist icon and message "No lists yet. Tap + to create one."

### `DashboardViewModel` (ChangeNotifier or Riverpod notifier)
- Exposes: `AsyncValue<List<TodoList>>` (or Stream)
- Methods: `deleteList(String id)`, `reorderLists(List<String> orderedIds)`

### `CreateListBottomSheet`
- Text field for list title (auto-focus, 50 char max)
- `ColorPickerRow` for color selection (pre-select first color)
- Save button calls `CreateListUseCase`, closes sheet on success
- Validates title is not empty

## Screen 2: List Detail (`lib/screens/list_detail/`)

### `ListDetailScreen`
- Route: `/list/:listId`
- Args: `String listId`
- AppBar title = list title with color dot leading
- Watches `watchItemsForList(listId)`
- Shows todo items as checkboxes (`AppCheckbox`)
- Tap checkbox: calls `ToggleItemUseCase`
- FAB or inline add: `AddItemTextField` (inline text field at bottom, auto-focus)
  - Pressing enter or Done calls `CreateItemUseCase`
- Swipe-to-delete on items (calls `DeleteItemUseCase`)
- Location chip at top (below AppBar): if list has geofenceId, show a `Chip` with pin icon + geofence label. Tapping navigates to Map Picker (Phase 2). For Phase 1, tapping shows a Snackbar "Location assignment coming soon"
- Empty state: `AppEmptyState` with checklist icon and "Add your first item below"

### `ListDetailViewModel`
- Exposes: `AsyncValue<TodoList>`, `AsyncValue<List<TodoItem>>`
- Methods: `toggleItem(String itemId)`, `createItem({required String title})`, `deleteItem(String id)`

## Navigation
Use `go_router: ^14.0.0` for routing.
- Routes: `/` → DashboardScreen, `/list/:listId` → ListDetailScreen
- Deep-link route `/list/:listId` for notification tap (Phase 3 will wire this up)

## Tests
Widget tests for:
- DashboardScreen: shows empty state when no lists, shows tiles when lists exist
- DashboardScreen: FAB opens create sheet
- ListDetailScreen: shows items, tap checkbox calls toggleItem
- ListDetailScreen: shows empty state when no items

## Acceptance criteria
- App runs on both iOS simulator and Android emulator
- CRUD flow works end-to-end: create list → add items → check items → delete list
- All tests pass
- `melos analyze` passes
title: Phase 1: List dashboard and list detail screens (no map)

close_reason: Scaffolded full monorepo: all packages created, pubspecs set, analysis_options using very_good_analysis, placeholder libs and passing tests in every package, iOS 14.0 + Android minSdk 23 configured. melos bootstrap/analyze/format/test all pass.
description:
## Overview
Set up the Flutter monorepo structure using melos. Create all package skeletons so subsequent tasks have the correct directory layout and `pubspec.yaml` stubs to build against.

## What already exists (do NOT recreate)
- `melos.yaml` at repo root — already fully configured with analyze/format/test/build/gen scripts
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — complete CI pipeline already in place
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — complete release pipeline already in place
- `.gitignore` at repo root

## What needs to be created
Directory/package structure:

```
apps/
  mobile/           ← Flutter app shell (iOS + Android targets)
packages/
  domain/           ← Pure Dart package, zero Flutter SDK dependency
  data/             ← Flutter plugin package (needs dart:io, sqflite FFI)
  geofencing/       ← Flutter plugin package (needs platform channels)
  notifications/    ← Flutter package (needs flutter_local_notifications)
  ui_kit/           ← Flutter package (widgets + theme)
```

## Steps

### 1. Create packages

```bash
# Pure Dart — domain must NOT depend on Flutter SDK
dart create --template=package packages/domain

# Flutter packages
flutter create --template=package packages/data
flutter create --template=package packages/geofencing
flutter create --template=package packages/notifications
flutter create --template=package packages/ui_kit

# App shell
flutter create --org com.yourcompany --project-name whileyoureout apps/mobile
```

### 2. Set pubspec.yaml for each package

**packages/domain/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: domain
description: Pure-Dart domain layer — entities, use cases, repository interfaces.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'

dependencies:
  equatable: ^2.0.5
  uuid: ^4.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
  test: ^1.24.0
```

**packages/data/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: data
description: SQLite data layer — drift schema and repository implementations.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  drift: ^2.18.0
  sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.5.0
  path_provider: ^2.1.0
  path: ^1.9.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  drift_dev: ^2.18.0
  build_runner: ^2.4.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/geofencing/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: geofencing
description: Geofence platform bridge — iOS CLLocationManager + Android GeofencingClient.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/notifications/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: notifications
description: Local notification service using flutter_local_notifications.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  flutter_local_notifications: ^17.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/ui_kit/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: ui_kit
description: Shared design system — theme, colors, and base widgets.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml** (key additions — merge with generated file)
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../../packages/domain
  data:
    path: ../../packages/data
  geofencing:
    path: ../../packages/geofencing
  notifications:
    path: ../../packages/notifications
  ui_kit:
    path: ../../packages/ui_kit
  flutter_riverpod: ^2.5.0
  go_router: ^14.0.0
  shared_preferences: ^2.2.0
  permission_handler: ^11.0.0
  flutter_map: ^7.0.0
  latlong2: ^0.9.0
  geolocator: ^12.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
```

### 3. Add analysis_options.yaml to every package
Each package needs `analysis_options.yaml` at its root:
```yaml
include: package:very_good_analysis/analysis_options.yaml
```

### 4. Add placeholder lib files
Each package needs a non-empty `lib/<name>.dart` barrel file so `melos analyze` does not error:
```dart
// lib/domain.dart
library domain;
```
(same pattern for data, geofencing, notifications, ui_kit)

### 5. Add placeholder tests
Each package needs `test/<name>_test.dart`:
```dart
void main() {}  // placeholder — real tests added in subsequent tasks
```

### 6. Verify workspace
```bash
melos bootstrap
melos analyze
melos format
melos test
```

All commands must pass before this task is considered complete.

## Key constraints
- `domain` package must use `dart create`, NOT `flutter create` — it must have zero Flutter SDK dependency
- Bundle ID for the app: `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`
- iOS deployment target: 14.0 (set in `apps/mobile/ios/Podfile`: `platform :ios, '14.0'`)
- Android minSdk: 23 (set in `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle`)
- The `melos.yaml` scope filters rely on the package `name` field matching exactly: 'domain', 'data', 'geofencing'

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos bootstrap` succeeds with no errors
- `melos analyze` passes across all packages
- `melos format` reports no changes needed
- `melos test` runs placeholder tests for all packages with a `test/` dir
- Directory structure exactly matches the layout above
title: Phase 1: Scaffold monorepo, melos config, and CI skeleton

description:
## Overview
Replace the current `flutter_map` (OpenStreetMap) implementation in `MapPickerScreen` with `google_maps_flutter`. Additionally, when the map picker opens for a list, query the Google Places Nearby Search API using the list's title as a keyword. If a matching place is found within 5 km of the user's current position, auto-center the map on it and pre-fill the location label. If no match is found the user picks manually and types a custom label.

## Affected files (apps/mobile)
- `pubspec.yaml` — remove flutter_map, add google_maps_flutter and http
- `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` — Maps API key manifest placeholder
- `android/app/build.gradle.kts` — read key from local.properties / CI env
- `ios/Runner/AppDelegate.swift` — GMSServices.provideAPIKey(...)
- `ios/Runner/Info.plist` — GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY entry (read from XCConfig)
- `lib/screens/map_picker/map_picker_screen.dart` — GoogleMap widget
- `lib/screens/map_picker/map_picker_view_model.dart` — Places suggestion state
- `lib/services/places_suggestion_service.dart` — NEW abstract interface
- `lib/services/google_places_suggestion_service.dart` — NEW HTTP implementation
- `test/screens/map_picker_view_model_test.dart` — update for suggestion state
- `docs/MAPS_SETUP.md` — NEW developer setup guide

## Key constraints
- The Google Maps API key must NEVER be committed. Use `--dart-define=GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=...` for Flutter code and manifest placeholders / XCConfig for native initialization.
- Network calls from the Places service are acceptable; only the app's own todo/geofence data must remain on-device per the epic privacy model (while-youre-out-1pq).
- `latlong2` stays in pubspec.yaml — it is still used by `MapPickerViewModel` and its tests. Only `flutter_map` is removed.
- `google_maps_flutter` on Android requires minSdkVersion >= 21 (current: 23, so fine) and compileSdk >= 34.
- iOS minimum deployment target must be 14.0+ for google_maps_flutter 2.x; verify `ios/Podfile` sets `platform :ios, '14.0'`.
- The Places Nearby Search uses the legacy REST endpoint: GET https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/nearbysearch/json
- The same API key must have "Maps SDK for Android", "Maps SDK for iOS", and "Places API" enabled in Google Cloud Console.

## Subtasks
- Sub-task 1: Configure google_maps_flutter dependency, API key plumbing, and developer docs
- Sub-task 2: Refactor MapPickerScreen to use GoogleMap widget
- Sub-task 3: Add Places Nearby Search auto-suggest to MapPickerScreen

## Acceptance criteria
1. Map picker shows Google Maps tiles (not OpenStreetMap)
2. Geofence circle and pin marker render correctly on Google Maps
3. Tapping the map places a pin; saving / removing a location works as before (no regression)
4. When the list title matches a nearby Google Place within 5 km, the map auto-centers and the label is pre-filled; a dismissible suggestion card is shown above the bottom sheet
5. When no nearby place matches, the map shows the current device location (or SF fallback) and the label is empty
6. `melos analyze` passes with zero issues
7. All existing MapPickerViewModel unit tests still pass
8. `docs/MAPS_SETUP.md` documents how to obtain and configure the API key locally and in CI
title: Add Google Maps to location picker with Places auto-suggest

description:
## Context
Part of epic: while-youre-out-t77 (Add Google Maps to location picker with Places auto-suggest).
This task sets up all dependency and native platform plumbing required before the screen refactor (sub2) and Places integration (sub3) can proceed. No Dart UI code changes in this task.

## 1. pubspec.yaml (`apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml`)

Remove:
```
flutter_map: ^7.0.0
```

Keep (still used by MapPickerViewModel and its tests):
```
latlong2: ^0.9.0
```

Add:
```
google_maps_flutter: ^2.9.0
http: ^1.2.0
```

After editing, run `flutter pub get` from `apps/mobile/` to verify resolution.

## 2. Android — API key manifest placeholder

### `apps/mobile/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`
Inside the `<application>` tag, add:
```xml
<meta-data
    android:name="com.google.android.geo.API_KEY"
    android:value="${GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY}"/>
```

### `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle.kts`
At the top of the file, after the existing `plugins { }` block, add code to load `local.properties`:
```kotlin
import java.util.Properties

val localProperties = Properties()
val localPropertiesFile = rootProject.file("local.properties")
if (localPropertiesFile.exists()) {
    localProperties.load(localPropertiesFile.inputStream())
}
```

Inside the existing `defaultConfig { ... }` block, add:
```kotlin
manifestPlaceholders["GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY"] =
    localProperties.getProperty("GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY")
        ?: System.getenv("GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY")
        ?: ""
```

`apps/mobile/android/local.properties` is already gitignored by the Android Gradle default `.gitignore`. Developers add their key there; CI injects it via an environment variable.

## 3. iOS — API key via XCConfig and AppDelegate

### 3a. XCConfig files
`apps/mobile/ios/Flutter/Debug.xcconfig` and `apps/mobile/ios/Flutter/Release.xcconfig` are Flutter-generated files that already exist and are imported by the Xcode project. Append the following line to BOTH files:
```
GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY = YOUR_KEY_HERE
```
These files are NOT gitignored (they contain Flutter-generated content that must be committed). Developers replace `YOUR_KEY_HERE` with their real key locally; CI injects the real value by overwriting the line or via a pre-build script (see docs/MAPS_SETUP.md).

### 3b. `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/Info.plist`
Add the following key/value pair so the value flows from the XCConfig build setting into the app bundle at build time:
```xml
<key>GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY</key>
<string>$(GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY)</string>
```
Place it alongside the existing `NSLocation*` usage description keys.

### 3c. `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/AppDelegate.swift`
The file currently contains the standard Flutter `AppDelegate`. Replace its contents with the version that initializes the Google Maps SDK before Flutter starts. The key is read from `Info.plist` (which received it from the XCConfig build setting):
```swift
import UIKit
import Flutter
import GoogleMaps

@UIApplicationMain
@objc class AppDelegate: FlutterAppDelegate {
  override func application(
    _ application: UIApplication,
    didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?
  ) -> Bool {
    let mapsKey = Bundle.main.object(
      forInfoDictionaryKey: "GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY"
    ) as? String ?? ""
    GMSServices.provideAPIKey(mapsKey)
    GeneratedPluginRegistrant.register(with: self)
    return super.application(application, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions: launchOptions)
  }
}
```
IMPORTANT: `GMSServices.provideAPIKey(_:)` MUST be called before `GeneratedPluginRegistrant.register(with: self)` and before `super.application(...)`.

### 3d. iOS Podfile minimum deployment target
Open `apps/mobile/ios/Podfile`. Ensure the platform line reads:
```ruby
platform :ios, '14.0'
```
`google_maps_flutter` 2.x requires iOS 14.0+. If the file currently specifies a lower version (e.g. `12.0`), update it. After changing, run `pod install` from `apps/mobile/ios/` to regenerate `Podfile.lock`.

## 4. Dart-define for Flutter-side key access

The Places API HTTP calls (implemented in sub3) will read the key at runtime via:
```dart
const String _googleMapsApiKey =
    String.fromEnvironment('GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY', defaultValue: '');
```
This constant is defined in the service implementation file (sub3), not here. This task only ensures the infrastructure is in place.

Developers run the app with:
```
flutter run --dart-define=GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY_HERE
```

## 5. CI workflow update

File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (at the repo root).

Locate every `flutter build` and `flutter test` step that runs against `apps/mobile` and append:
```yaml
--dart-define=GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=${{ secrets.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY }}
```

For Android Gradle steps (e.g. `./gradlew assembleDebug`), add the key to the job's `env:` block:
```yaml
env:
  GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY }}
```
This ensures the manifest placeholder resolves during Android builds in CI.

Add `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` to the list of required secrets in the CI README or in `docs/MAPS_SETUP.md` (see below).

## 6. New file: `docs/MAPS_SETUP.md`

Create this file at the repo root (`docs/MAPS_SETUP.md`) with the following content:

```
# Google Maps & Places API Setup

## Google Cloud Console — one-time setup

1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/
2. Create or select a project.
3. Enable these APIs on a single API key:
   - Maps SDK for Android
   - Maps SDK for iOS
   - Places API
4. Under "API restrictions", restrict the key to only those three APIs.
5. Under "Application restrictions":
   - For Android: restrict to the app's SHA-1 fingerprint and package name (`com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`)
   - For iOS: restrict to the bundle ID (`com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`)
   (Use separate keys per platform in production for stricter security.)

## Android local setup

Add your key to `apps/mobile/android/local.properties` (this file is gitignored):
```
GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY_HERE
```

## iOS local setup

Edit `apps/mobile/ios/Flutter/Debug.xcconfig` and replace the placeholder:
```
GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY = YOUR_KEY_HERE
```

Do the same in `apps/mobile/ios/Flutter/Release.xcconfig`.

## Running the app locally

Always pass the key as a dart-define so the Places API (used from Dart code) can access it:
```
cd apps/mobile
flutter run --dart-define=GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY_HERE
```

## CI / GitHub Actions

Add `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` as a repository secret in GitHub:
Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret.

The CI workflows (`ci.yml`, `release.yml`) already pass it via `--dart-define` and Android `env:` after this task is merged.

## Verifying the Android key resolved correctly

After a debug build:
```
cat apps/mobile/build/app/outputs/apk/debug/output-metadata.json
# or inspect the merged manifest:
cat apps/mobile/android/app/build/intermediates/merged_manifests/debug/AndroidManifest.xml | grep GOOGLE_MAPS
```
The value should NOT be an empty string or literal `${GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY}`.
```

## Acceptance criteria
- `flutter pub get` in `apps/mobile/` succeeds with the new dependencies
- `melos analyze` passes with zero issues across all packages
- `docs/MAPS_SETUP.md` exists and covers all setup steps listed above
- `apps/mobile/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` contains the `com.google.android.geo.API_KEY` meta-data entry
- `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/AppDelegate.swift` calls `GMSServices.provideAPIKey(...)` before registering plugins
- `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/Info.plist` contains the `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` key reading from build settings
- No API key value appears in any committed file (only placeholders like `YOUR_KEY_HERE`)
- CI workflow files updated to pass `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` secret to build and test steps
title: Configure google_maps_flutter dependency, API key plumbing, and developer docs
Refactor MapPickerScreen to use GoogleMap widget (replace flutter_map)
Add Places Nearby Search auto-suggest to MapPickerScreen

description:
Discovered while implementing while-youre-out-3r5 (domain package).

The melos.yaml test:domain script runs 'flutter test --coverage' but domain is a pure Dart package with no Flutter SDK dependency. 'flutter test' fails on pure Dart packages. Fix: change test:domain (and test:data if also pure Dart) to use 'dart test --coverage' instead.

Additionally, the coverage:combine script relies on 'dart pub global run coverage:format_coverage' which is not activated globally in the environment, so lcov.info is never populated after dart test runs. Either activate the coverage package in CI (dart pub global activate coverage) or adopt an alternative lcov generation approach.

The epic this is part of: while-youre-out-1pq.
title: Fix melos test:domain script to use dart test for pure-Dart package

close_reason: Implemented FlutterNotificationService (flutter_local_notifications), GeofenceEventHandler, provider wiring, main.dart startup, and unit tests. melos analyze passes, 19 tests pass.
title: Phase 3: notifications package and geofence-entry → notification pipeline
